# RoExpress

> A type-safe, rate-limited, Express.js-style networking framework for Roblox. MIT licensed and free to use.

## What is RoExpress?

RoExpress is the first complete networking stack for Roblox. It covers every layer and every direction of game networking in one framework:

- **Inbound** — typed route matching (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE), middleware pipeline, per-player rate limiting, exploit detection, named isolated ports
- **Outbound** — Harpy HTTP client for server-to-external-API calls with retry and a consistent response shape
- **Server push** — reliable server-initiated messages to individual players or all clients
- **Real-time streaming** — Stream schema-defined binary channels with delta compression and lag compensation; packets 40-90% smaller than JSON
- **Utilities** — Deflate compression (Codec), Promise-based async API, TypeCoercer, Bridge internal event bus, Benchmark latency tooling

No other Roblox networking library covers all of these layers. Previous libraries (ByteNet, Warp, BridgeNet2) are single-layer transports; Zap is a serialisation code generator. RoExpress is a framework — it owns the complete request lifecycle from client call to server handler to external API.

## Current Version

2.4.0 — released 2026-06-09

## What's new in v2.4

- **Deflate compression**: Codec upgraded from LZ77 to full Deflate (LZ77 sliding window + Huffman entropy coding). Better compression ratios on repetitive payloads, same opt-in API ({ compress = true }).
- **Automatic retry**: Network requests automatically retry with exponential backoff (1s → 2s → 4s) on failure. Configurable max attempts globally or per-request. Transparent to the caller.
- **Stream — generic channels**: Stream rebuilt as schema-defined typed channels. Declare field names and types; Stream handles buffer pack/unpack automatically. Works for any high-frequency state (positions, economy, weather) — not only FPS shooters. Delta compression built in.
- **Typed accessors**: RoExpress.GetApp() and RoExpress.GetNetwork() return fully-typed instances. The existing RoExpress("App") / RoExpress("Network") call style still works and returns `any`.

## Modules

- **Harpy**: Server-only outbound HTTP client. Wraps HttpService:RequestAsync with a persistent base URL, shared headers, automatic retry with exponential backoff, and a consistent response shape { ok, status, data, headers, raw }. Factory pattern — create one client per external API. Callback style (:Get/:Post/:Put/:Delete) and chainable Promise style (:GetAsync etc.).
- **App**: Server-side request routing, middleware pipeline, and reliable server push (Push/PushAll/PushTo). Owns a Router and TokenBucket instance. Handlers use compact (req, res) form; req.player and req.raw available when needed.
- **Network**: Client-side request/response with callbacks and optional timeout. Automatic retry with exponential backoff. Exposes GetAsync/PostAsync returning chainable Promises.
- **Broadcast**: Unreliable server-to-client fire-and-forget events over UnreliableRemoteEvent. Per-event and per-player rate limiting. 900 byte data cap.
- **Listener**: Client-side handler for both unreliable broadcast and reliable server push through one API (On/Once/Off). Transparently separates push packets from request-response packets.
- **Router**: Typed route matching with param types: string, number, int, boolean, vector2, vector3, color3, cframe, Enum.TypeName, Instance. Wildcards, globs, and inline pattern constraints. Routes sorted by specificity — most specific always wins.
- **Codec**: Deflate compression (LZ77 sliding window + Huffman entropy coding). Opt-in per route via { compress = true }. Typical savings 40–70% on repetitive JSON payloads. Auto-decompressed on client before callback fires.
- **Bridge**: Shared singleton internal event bus for server-side module decoupling. Bind/Fire/Wait/WaitUntil/WaitFirst/BindOnce. Does not cross the client/server boundary.
- **Tamper**: Server-only passive exploit detection. Hooks into App's pipeline. Multiple detection tiers: malformed payload, version spoof, rate flood, oversized body. AutoKick, manual Strike, configurable thresholds and kick message.
- **Port**: Named isolated request pipelines via Port.New(name). Each Port owns its own RemoteEvent, Router, and TokenBucket. Use when a system needs separate rate limiting and middleware from the main App.
- **Stream**: Schema-defined typed binary channels. Declare a schema at construction; Stream handles packing and unpacking. One buffer, one FireAllClients per tick regardless of record count. Delta compression — only changed records sent. Built-in lag compensation via ch:Rewind(userId, timestamp) for hit validation.
- **TypeCoercer**: Shared type serialisation singleton. TC.ToString converts Luau/Roblox types to wire strings; TC.FromString coerces them back. Supports: number, int, boolean, string, Vector2, Vector3, CFrame, Color3, UDim, UDim2, Rect, BrickColor, Enum member, Instance path. Powers Router param coercion.
- **Promise**: Lightweight chainable async API for Network. Then/Catch/Finally/Cancel. Returned by GetAsync and PostAsync.
- **TokenBucket**: Per-instance RFC 2697 token bucket rate limiter. App and Broadcast own independent instances. Grant/GrantAll/Reset utilities for rewarding active players.
- **Benchmark**: Client-side round-trip latency measurement. Run(route, config?), results include min/avg/median/p95/p99/max/errors/throughput. Print and Compare helpers.
- **Base64**: Shared encode/decode utility. Used internally for binary POST responses.

## Request pipeline (in order)

1. Version check — Tamper discards packets with a mismatched framework version (silent drop)
2. TokenBucket — per-player rate limiter consumes one token (429 on empty)
3. Payload validation — method, route, and body shape checked (400 / Tamper strike)
4. Global middleware — each Use handler runs; return false stops the chain (403)
5. Route match — Router finds the first matching pattern and extracts typed params (404)
6. Route middleware — any Use scoped to this route (403)
7. Handler — your code runs; res:Send() or res:Error() fires the response (500 on unhandled error)

## Installation

Wally (recommended):
```toml
[dependencies]
RoExpress = "unofficialrobloxtutor/roexpress@2.4.0"
```

Then: `wally install`

Manual: download from GitHub and place the ModuleScript in ReplicatedStorage.

## Comparison to other Roblox networking libraries

- **vs ByteNet**: ByteNet provides buffer serialization only and is unmaintained. RoExpress includes buffer serialization (Stream + Codec) plus routing, middleware, rate limiting, server push, exploit detection, and named ports.
- **vs Zap**: Zap generates typed serialization code at build time. No routing, no security, no push, no rate limiting. Good for maximum serialization performance; RoExpress is better for full-stack request architecture.
- **vs BridgeNet2**: BridgeNet2 provided identifier batching but no routing, no security, no push. Archived.
- **vs Warp**: Warp provided fast remotes but no routing structure, no security, no push. Archived.

## Language

Luau (Roblox's scripting language — a typed superset of Lua 5.1)

MIT — free to use, modify, and distribute.
